step_by_step_reasoning: The whiteboard shows a direct flow of TV telemetry data from connected devices into AWS. The sequence is: TV -> Kinesis -> Kinesis Firehose -> S3 and Redshift, followed by QuickSight visualization from Redshift. We map the TV as UserConsumerTV, followed by canonical AWS services: Kinesis, Firehose, S3, RedShift, and QuickSight.

graph.name: Vizio Telemetry Analytics Pipeline
graph.notes: Ingests billions of telemetry events monthly from millions of connected TVs. Uses Kinesis Streams and Firehose for real-time buffering, storing raw data long-term in S3, structured data in Redshift, and visualizing with QuickSight.

nodes.name: Vizio Smart TVs
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Millions of connected devices, billions of telemetry events (logs, performance, user data) per month.

nodes.name: Kinesis Data Streams
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Real-time ingestion front door configured with an 8-shard pipeline.

nodes.name: Kinesis Data Firehose
nodes.notes: Handles ingestion as-is without requiring upfront schema enforcement.

nodes.name: Amazon S3
nodes.notes: Long-term raw data store for future reuse, migration buffer, and historical archives.

nodes.name: Amazon Redshift
nodes.notes: Columnar data warehouse optimized for massive aggregations over billions of rows.

nodes.name: Amazon QuickSight
nodes.notes: Provides interactive dashboards using Spice in-memory engine and custom SQL.

edges.notes: Sends raw log, performance, and user telemetry directly to Kinesis.
Persists raw data long-term in S3.
Queries data via custom SQL into QuickSight Spice engine.
